2603.25638.title: Beyond Via: Analysis and Estimation of the Impact of Large Language Models in Academic Papers
2603.25638.paper_summary: Through an analysis of arXiv papers, we report several shifts in word usage that are likely driven by large language models (LLMs) but have not previously received sufficient attention, such as the increased frequency of "beyond" and "via" in titles and the decreased frequency of "the" and "of" in abstracts. Due to the similarities among different LLMs, experiments show that current classifiers struggle to accurately determine which specific model generated a given text in multi-class classification tasks. Meanwhile, variations across LLMs also result in evolving patterns of word usage in academic papers. By adopting a direct and highly interpretable linear approach and accounting for differences between models and prompts, we quantitatively assess these effects and show that real-world LLM usage is heterogeneous and dynamic.
2603.25638.paper_summary_zh: 透過對 arXiv 論文的分析，我們報告了幾個可能由大型語言模型（LLMs）驅動的詞彙使用變化，但這些變化之前並未受到足夠的關注，例如在標題中「beyond」和「via」的使用頻率增加，以及在摘要中「the」和「of」的使用頻率減少。由於不同 LLM 之間的相似性，實驗顯示目前的分類器在多類別分類任務中難以準確判斷特定文本是由哪個模型生成的。與此同時，LLM 之間的變異也導致學術論文中詞彙使用模式的演變。通過採用直接且高度可解釋的線性方法，並考慮模型和提示之間的差異，我們定量評估這些影響，並顯示現實世界中 LLM 的使用是異質且動態的。
2603.25638.author: Mingmeng Geng et.al.
2603.25638.authors: Mingmeng Geng, Yuhang Dong, Thierry Poibeau
